data.title: Liminatus Pharma stock gains on regaining compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• Liminatus Pharma (NASDAQ:LIMN) shares rose after the company said it regained compliance with Nasdaq's listing needs.
  • The stock is up 43.5%.
  • The company, last month, had received a notice of non-compliance from the stock exchange.
  • On 2nd June, Liminatus filed its 10-Q, confirming its doubt in the ability to continue as a going concern.

data.title: Lynas shares surge to two-year high on risks from China's rare earth export restrictions
data.description:
Lynas (OTCPK:LYSCF) (OTCPK:LYSDY) +10% in Thursday's trading to their highest level since February 2023 after global automakers warned that China's export restrictions on rare earth materials could lead to production delays, according to Reuters.

Analysts said Lynas (OTCPK:LYSCF) (OTCPK:LYSDY), as the world's largest rare earth producing company outside China, could benefit from the conditions in an environment of rising geopolitical tension and demand for critical minerals.

"As China tightens rare-earth export controls, markets are pricing in supply risks, positioning Lynas... as a strategic hedge," Vantage Markets analyst Hebe Chen told Reuters.

China, which accounts for 90% of global rare earth production, imposed export restrictions in April on the strategic minerals in response to President Trump's tariffs, raising alarms across industries reliant on the elements that are critical for defense systems, electric vehicles, clean energy, and advanced electronics.

This week, German automakers added urgency to the concerns, warning that China's export restrictions on rare earths pose a significant threat to their production lines and local economies.

data.title: Market Voices: Musk attacks GOP spending bill; ECB cuts interest rates
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's daily roundup of statements and remarks by newsmakers that might impact markets, sectors or individual stocks.

-Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO and Trump supporter Elon Musk continued to criticize the GOP-backed budget bill currently making its way through Congress.

"A new spending bill should be drafted that doesn’t massively grow the deficit and increase the debt ceiling by 5 TRILLION DOLLARS," Musk posted on X on Wednesday afternoon. 

Musk later posted, "Call your Senator, Call your Congressman, Bankrupting America is NOT ok! KILL the BILL."

-The European Central Bank said it was lowering three key interest rates by 25 basis points, bringing the deposit facility rate down to 2%. It added that inflation was now at around its 2% medium-term target. 

"The decision to lower the deposit facility rate – the rate through which the Governing Council steers the monetary policy stance – is based on its updated assessment of the inflation outlook, the dynamics of underlying inflation and the strength of monetary policy transmission,” the ECB said in a statement on Thursday. 

-Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said at a NATO meeting on Thursday that the U.S. is expecting member states to raise their individual contributions to 5% of their respective GDPs. Currently, members are only required to contribute 2%, with several falling short of that goal in recent years.

“We’re here to continue the work that President Trump started, which is a commitment to 5% defense spending across this alliance, which, we think, will happen,” Hegseth told reporters, according to CNBC.

data.title: Arlo Technologies stock soars on, surpassing $300M in ARR
data.description:
• Arlo Technologies (NYSE:ARLO) has now joined the ranks of elite public companies by surpassing $300M in annual recurring revenue, driven by continued growth from subscription services delivered by Arlo's AI-powered smart security platform.
  • Underpinning this growth is Arlo's globally-scaled SaaS platform with Arlo Intelligence™, Arlo's groundbreaking AI engine that enables customers to activate, tailor and manage their smart security ecosystem that adapts and grows as needs change.
  • "Today's announcement is the result of the tireless pursuit and exceptional focus of everyone at Arlo committed to making smart security accessible for anyone, at any stage of life." said Matthew McRae, CEO.
  • Arlo continues to outpace its peers through its relentless commitment to innovation and operational excellence.
  • In commemoration of Arlo's milestone achievement, Arlo has been invited to host the opening bell at the NYSE on July 9, 2025.
  • ARLO is +6.99% to $15.61.

data.title: Trump says phone call with China's Xi 'resulted in a very positive conclusion'
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday said that he had held a "good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the world's two largest economies. Trump said there "should no longer be any questions" over rare earths, and that he had been invited by Xi to visit China. 

"The conversation was focused almost entirely on TRADE. Nothing was discussed concerning Russia/Ukraine, or Iran. We will inform the Media as to scheduling and location of the soon to be meeting," the President said on his Truth Social service. 

In the morning, Xinhua and CNN had reported that the two Presidents were holding a phone call. Xinhua added that the call had been requested by Trump. 

The White House responded to an earlier request for comment from Seeking Alpha with Trump's Truth Social post.      

U.S. stock index futures rose sharply on Xinhua's report, but quickly gave up those gains. Stocks have fluctuated after the opening bell. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

And here are some China-linked ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), and (NYSEARCA:YINN).

  • Forward yield 7.3%

data.title: Circle shares indicated to open as much as 42% above IPO price - report
data.description:
Circle Internet Group (NYSE:CRCL) stock is indicated to debut at $42-$44 per share on Thursday, representing a 36%-42% premium relative to the $31 initial public offering price, Bloomberg reported.

The stablecoin issuer and some of its backers raised $1.1B in the offering, selling 34M shares for $31 each. That was upsized from the previous marketed range of $27-$28 after being boosted once before amid strong demand.

Shares are slated to start trading Thursday on the New York Stock Exchange.

Circle (NYSE:CRCL) is the issuer of USD Coin (USDC-USD), the second-largest stablecoin by market cap.

A stablecoin is a type of crypto designed to maintain a stable value by being pegged to a real-world asset like the U.S. dollar or gold.

data.title: Harmony Biosciences, Lupin reach agreement on Wakix generic launch
data.description:
• Lupin can launch a generic version of Harmony Biosciences' (NASDAQ:HRMY) Wakix (pitolisant) in January 2030 under a settlement agreement.
  • The agreement resolves patent infringement litigation filed by Harmony against Lupin regarding its ANDA for generic Wakix, a treatment for excessive daytime sleepiness.
  • Harmony noted that it is currently working on new formulations of pitolisant with utility patents that could provide exclusivity through 2044.

data.title: ScottsMiracle-Gro reaffirms fiscal 2025 guidance; shares up over 10%
data.description:
• Shares of The Scotts Miracle-Gro Company jumped over 10% on Thursday after the company reaffirmed its full fiscal year 2025 guidance. 
  • The company expects U.S. consumer net sales low single-digit growth, excluding non-repeat sales for AeroGarden and bulk raw material sales. 
  • The company expects non-GAAP adjusted gross margin of approximately 30 percent and non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA of $570 million to $590 million and free cash flow of approximately $250 million.
  • The company also projects interest expense to be approximately $30 million lower than the prior year compared to its previous estimate of a $15 million to $20 million decrease, and it projects diluted share count increase to be approximately 1 million compared to its previous estimate of 2 million.
  • The company expects non-GAAP adjusted earnings per diluted share to be at least $3.50 vs. consensus of $3.47, a 53% increase versus prior year.
  • Chief Financial Officer Mark Scheiwer said, “The strong consumer takeaway, along with the pace of retailer replenishment thus far in the third quarter, solidifies our confidence in the full-year guidance. Delivering on our adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow targets will enable us to exit 2025 with a significantly improved debt position that will put us on a path to realize our goal of getting leverage below 3.5 by the end of fiscal 2027.”
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Qualcomm completes acquisition of Autotalks after overcoming FTC roadblocks
data.description:
Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) has completed its acquisition of Autotalks, a developer of direct vehicle-to-everything communication solutions, after overcoming roadblocks from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission's Bureau of Competition when it tried to acquire the company last year.

Autotalks' V2X technologies are used to reduce collisions by enabling vehicles to communicate directly with nearby vehicles and the surrounding environment. The financial details of the acquisition were not disclosed.

"We've been investing in V2X technologies since 2017, recognizing their potential to improve road safety," said Nakul Duggal, general manager of Automotive and Industrial & Embedded IoT at Qualcomm. "This acquisition is a strategic step in our ongoing efforts, advancing V2X solutions further for connected and automated vehicles, while driving global deployment, and strengthening our Snapdragon Digital Chassis product portfolio."

"The combination of our expertise and industry-leading V2X products with Qualcomm Technologies' global automotive footprint and strong momentum among automakers positions us well to accelerate V2X developments and adoption across the globe," added Hagai Zyss, former CEO of Autotalks, who is now vice president of Product Management at Qualcomm. 

Last year, the FTC probed Qualcomm's proposed acquisition of Autotalks, prompting Qualcomm to back out of the deal.

"The termination of this proposed transaction will preserve vigorous innovation and price competition in the market for vehicle-to-everything chipsets and related products used in automotive safety systems," said Henry Liu in March 2024, who was then director of the FTC's Bureau of Competition.
